senses.definition: paying much attention to something or someone that makes one unaware of other things

examples.x: She was so absorbed in her book that she didn't notice the time passing and missed her bus stop.

senses.definition: having a desire to learn many different things and asks many questions to gain knowledge or understanding

examples.x: The inquisitive child always has a multitude of questions about how things work.
She is known for her inquisitive nature, constantly seeking out new information and experiences.
The inquisitive journalist conducted thorough research to uncover the truth behind the story.
His inquisitive mind led him to pursue a career in science, where he could explore the mysteries of the universe.
The inquisitive traveler enjoys immersing themselves in different cultures, eager to learn about new customs and traditions.

word_list.pronunciation: 藢忙p晒i藞h蓻ns瑟v/, /藢忙p晒瑟藞h蓻ns瑟v

senses.definition: nervous or worried that something unpleasant may happen

examples.x: She felt apprehensive about the upcoming job interview.
He was apprehensive about the dentist appointment, fearing it would be painful.
The students were apprehensive before taking their final exams.
He felt apprehensive about meeting his girlfriend's parents for the first time.
The team was apprehensive about the new project's challenging deadline.

senses.definition: greatly shocked or surprised

examples.x: The scientist was astounded to discover a new species of insect in the Amazon rainforest.
The teacher was astounded at the creativity and depth of thought in the student's project, awarding it the highest marks.

senses.definition: very nervous in a way that makes one unable to think clearly

examples.x: She became agitated when she realized she had forgotten her presentation notes.
The dog became agitated during the thunderstorm and couldn't sit still.
He grew agitated as the deadline for the project approached and he hadn't finished his work.
She felt agitated before her job interview, making it difficult for her to focus on her preparation.
The students grew agitated as the teacher announced a surprise quiz, fearing they hadn't studied enough.

senses.definition: greatly frightened and worried about something, in a way that is uncontrollable

examples.x: She became frantic when she couldn't find her child in the crowded mall.
His frantic search for his lost wallet turned the house upside down.
The dog's frantic barking alerted the family to the approaching intruder.
As the deadline approached, she grew frantic trying to finish her project on time.
His frantic pacing back and forth showed his anxiety before the big job interview.

senses.definition: physically or mentally weakened as a result of much suffering

examples.x: The long, grueling divorce left her feeling broken, questioning if she would ever be able to trust again.

senses.definition: causing a strong feeling of worry or discomfort

examples.x: The disturbing images in the horror movie lingered in her mind long after it ended.
The disturbing behavior of the neighbor's dog made her wary of going outside.
The disturbing sound of footsteps outside her window made her feel anxious.
The disturbing realization that someone had been stalking her sent chills down her spine.

word_list.pronunciation: d瑟s藞伞蓹st蓹d/, /d瑟s藞伞蓹st瑟d

senses.definition: having or displaying great dislike for something

examples.x: He felt disgusted by the dishonesty of his colleague.
The disgusted expression on her face revealed her distaste for the offensive joke.
She felt disgusted by the dirty conditions of the public restroom.
The disgusted customers left the restaurant after finding a cockroach in their food.

senses.definition: possessing traits that attract people's affection

examples.x: The puppy's playful nature and wagging tail made it instantly lovable to everyone in the family.
Her kind and compassionate personality made her a lovable friend to all.
The character's quirky charm and sense of humor made them a lovable protagonist in the story.
The kitten's soft fur and gentle purring made it irresistibly lovable to its owner.
The rescue dog's grateful demeanor and eager tail wags made it a lovable addition to the family.

word_list.pronunciation: 藞d蓻s蓹藢色e瑟t/, /藞d蓻s蓹色蓹t/, /藞d蓻z蓹色蓹t

senses.definition: feeling very lonely and sad

examples.x: After her best friend moved away, she felt desolate and struggled to find joy in her daily routine.
The desolate widow sat by the window, staring out at the empty street with tears in her eyes.
He wandered through the desolate town, feeling the weight of his loneliness pressing down on him.
The holidays always made her feel desolate, as she missed her family who lived far away.
In the desolate aftermath of the breakup, he found it hard to imagine ever feeling happy again.

senses.definition: devoid of respect for someone or something

examples.x: She gave him a contemptuous look after his rude comment.
He responded to the suggestion with a contemptuous sneer.
He made a contemptuous remark about her choice of career.
His contemptuous attitude towards authority often got him in trouble.
Her contemptuous laughter made him feel small and insignificant.

senses.definition: satisfied and happy with one's current situation

examples.x: Despite facing challenges, he remained content with his modest lifestyle.

senses.definition: expressing much attention and love toward someone or something

examples.x: She was a devoted mother, always putting her children's needs above her own.
He remained devoted to his craft, spending hours practicing and perfecting his skills.
Despite the challenges, she remained devoted to her passion for environmental conservation.
The caregiver showed devoted care to the elderly residents, treating them with kindness and patience.
The dog was devoted to its owner, following them everywhere and eagerly awaiting their return home.

senses.definition: extremely excited and happy

examples.x: She was ecstatic when she found out she had won the lottery, unable to contain her excitement.
He was ecstatic after receiving the job offer of his dreams.
The children were ecstatic when they found out they were going to Disneyland.
She was ecstatic to see her favorite band perform live in concert.
The couple was ecstatic upon learning they were expecting their first child.

senses.definition: feeling intense excitement or pleasure

examples.x: Winning the championship made the team members feel absolutely thrilled.

senses.definition: experiencing or expressing sadness or a general sense of unhappiness

examples.x: She felt gloomy after hearing the disappointing news.
His gloomy mood cast a shadow over the entire team.
The gloomy man rarely smiled and often kept to himself.
He had a gloomy expression after hearing the bad news.

senses.definition: causing great happiness

examples.x: The joyful music lifted everyone's spirits at the party.
The joyful news of her promotion spread quickly among her colleagues.
The joyful celebration lasted well into the night, filled with dancing and laughter.
The joyful celebration continued with fireworks lighting up the sky.
The joyful reunion with her family brought tears to her eyes.

senses.definition: feeling or showing emotional attachment or nostalgia toward a person or thing

examples.x: Whenever she visited her grandparents' house, she felt a warm sense of fond nostalgia, surrounded by familiar objects and family stories from years gone by.
He spoke with fond affection about his favorite childhood book, which he still kept on his shelf.
She cherished the handwritten letters from her pen pal, feeling a fond connection despite never having met in person.
The old photographs in the album filled her with fond memories of family gatherings and vacations.
The cozy little caf茅 on the corner was a place of fond memories for the locals, who gathered there for coffee and conversation.
With a fond smile, he recalled the days spent playing with his loyal childhood dog in the backyard.

senses.definition: very scared and shocked by something unpleasant or bad
very scared or shocked

examples.x: He felt horrified when he stumbled upon the scene of the accident.
The horrified expression on her face revealed her shock at the gruesome discovery.
She felt horrified by the thought of encountering a ghost in the abandoned house.

senses.definition: feeling disappointed because someone or something is not as worthy or good as one believed

examples.x: After working in politics for years, he became disillusioned with the system's corruption and inefficiency.
She felt disillusioned when her dream job turned out to be much less fulfilling than she had imagined.
After investing all her savings in the business venture, she became disillusioned when it failed to generate any profit.
He became disillusioned with his idol after learning about the celebrity's unethical behavior behind the scenes.

senses.definition: feeling extreme anxiety or discomfort

examples.x: She appeared distressed after receiving the news of her failing grades.
He felt distressed by the loss of his beloved pet.
The distressed mother searched frantically for her missing child.
She felt distressed by the conflict between her friends.

senses.definition: feeling very upset or nervous

examples.x: The disturbed expression on her face revealed her unease.

senses.definition: feeling nervous or worried, especially about something unpleasant that might happen soon

examples.x: She felt uneasy about the upcoming storm, fearing the potential damage it might cause.
He felt uneasy about the looming deadline, unsure if he would be able to finish the project on time.
She was uneasy about the unfamiliar surroundings, feeling anxious about her safety.
They were uneasy about the sudden change in plans, feeling unsettled by the lack of communication.
He was uneasy about the strange noises coming from the basement, fearing there might be an intruder.

senses.definition: having or displaying aggressiveness

examples.x: The fierce tiger bared its teeth and growled at the intruder in its territory.
The fierce competitor did not back down, even when challenged by his rivals.
Their fierce response to the criticism made it clear they were not willing to compromise.
In the animal kingdom, a fierce predator often commands respect and fear from other creatures.
The team's fierce attitude on the field was evident as they aggressively challenged every play.

senses.definition: displaying a cold or unwelcoming demeanor

examples.x: Her frozen expression revealed her discomfort in the crowded room.
He offered a frozen greeting, making it clear he wasn't in the mood for small talk.
She wore a frozen smile that didn't reach her eyes, masking her true feelings.
The frozen reaction from the audience suggested they were unimpressed by the performance.

senses.definition: feeling upset or annoyed due to being unable to do or achieve something

examples.x: She felt frustrated after spending hours trying to fix the broken computer.
They felt frustrated by the lack of progress in resolving the ongoing conflict.
She became frustrated when her efforts to communicate were misunderstood.
He grew frustrated with his slow progress in learning a new language.
They grew increasingly frustrated with the repeated delays.

senses.definition: wanting to know more about something because it seems very interesting
displaying or having a desire to protect someone or something

examples.x: The mother bear was fiercely protective of her cubs, keeping a close watch on them at all times.
He felt protective of his younger sister, always ready to defend her from any harm.
The security guard was vigilant and protective of the museum's priceless artifacts.
She wore a protective mask and gloves while handling chemicals in the laboratory.
The organization was dedicated to the protective care of endangered species in the wild.

senses.definition: causing strong reactions or discussions by presenting controversial or thought-provoking ideas

examples.x: The provocative artwork sparked heated debates about freedom of expression.
The politician's provocative statements ignited outrage among certain groups.
The provocative article sparked intense debate among readers.
Using provocative language in a debate can be effective in capturing the audience's attention.
His provocative question during the discussion challenged everyone's assumptions and sparked lively debate.

senses.definition: embarrassed or worried about one's appearance or actions

examples.x: She felt self-conscious about her new haircut, worried that it didn't suit her face shape.
He became self-conscious when all eyes turned to him during the presentation, causing him to stumble over his words.
As a teenager, she was incredibly self-conscious about her braces and avoided smiling in public.
He felt self-conscious about his accent when speaking in front of native speakers of the language.
The actress was surprisingly self-conscious about her performance, despite receiving rave reviews from critics.

word_list.pronunciation: 藢s蓻n蓹藞m蓻n蓹色/, /藢s蓻n蓹藞m蓻nt蓹色/, /藢s蓻nt蓹藞m蓻n蓹色/, /藢s蓻nt蓹藞m蓻nt蓹色

senses.definition: easily affected by emotions

examples.x: She is sentimental about the old photographs because they remind her of cherished memories with her family.
He grew sentimental when he found his childhood teddy bear tucked away in the attic.
The elderly couple became sentimental as they reminisced about their first date on their 50th wedding anniversary.
She has a sentimental attachment to the antique necklace because it was passed down from her grandmother.
He tends to get sentimental during holidays, reflecting on past celebrations and traditions with loved ones.

senses.definition: unable to speak for a short time, particularly as a result of surprise, shock, or anger

examples.x: She was left speechless when she saw the breathtaking view from the mountaintop.
She was speechless when he got down on one knee and proposed.
The news of her promotion left her speechless with disbelief.
The beauty of the sunset rendered him speechless for a moment.

senses.definition: great sympathy for a person or animal that is suffering

examples.x: Her compassion for stray animals led her to volunteer at the local animal shelter every weekend.
The doctor showed great compassion towards her elderly patients, taking extra time to listen to their concerns.
In times of crisis, communities often come together to support each other with compassion and generosity.
Teaching children about compassion and empathy is essential for building a caring and inclusive society.
His compassion for the homeless inspired him to start a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing shelter and resources.

senses.definition: a feeling of great distress or sadness

examples.x: Experiencing heartbreak after the end of a long-term relationship can be devastating.
She felt a deep sense of heartbreak when she learned of her friend's sudden passing.
Witnessing the destruction caused by the natural disaster brought a sense of heartbreak to the entire community.
The novel's poignant ending left readers with a lingering sense of heartbreak.

word_list.pronunciation: k藞蕦m t蓹 t藞蓽藧mz w瑟冒 藢蓻st藢i藧藞e瑟t蕛

senses.definition: to gradually learn to accept or deal with something unpleasant

examples.x: It took Jane a while to come to terms with her divorce, but eventually, she found happiness again.
Tom struggled to come to terms with his diagnosis, but with time, he learned to manage his condition.
The company had to come to terms with the reality of a shrinking market and adapt their business strategy.
Coming to terms with the loss of a beloved pet can be a deeply emotional experience.
Mary needed to come to terms with her past mistakes in order to move forward and build a better future.

senses.definition: used for showing annoyance or anger with a person or thing, in a way that is not very polite
used for expressing annoyance or anger in a way that may seem a bit offensive
experiencing an state of extreme anger, excitement, or craziness

word_list.pronunciation: d瑟s瑟n藞t蕛忙n瑟d/, /d瑟s瑟n藞t蕛忙nt瑟d

senses.definition: not believing in the worth or value of a person or thing any longer

examples.x: After years of working in corporate finance, she became disenchanted with the industry's focus on profit over people.
The voters grew disenchanted with the politician's promises after seeing little progress on key issues.
Many employees became disenchanted with the company's management after several rounds of layoffs.
He felt disenchanted with the art world after struggling to find recognition for his work.
